title: Formal jet equations
summary: Defines a syntax tree for the symbols in the Juhl obstruction recursion and assembles the formal coefficient equation Lₘ from its highest-jet, trace-derivative, Laplacian-variation, and zeroth-order trace terms.

mathematicalContent.heading: Formal syntax
mathematicalContent.body: FormalExpr is not a space of geometric functions. It is an expression language containing rational constants, k, boundary data f, normal jets Fₘ, trace jets trⱼ, sums, products, negation, and formal operators Dⱼ.
mathematicalContent.formula: E::=0\mid1\mid q\mid k\mid f\mid F_m\mid\operatorname{tr}_j\mid E+E\mid-E\mid EE\mid D_j(E).

mathematicalContent.heading: Coefficient equation
mathematicalContent.body: The distinguished coefficient of Fₘ₊₁ in Lₘ is 4(m+1−ℓ)/m!. All remaining terms are assembled from binomial Taylor factors.

mathematicalContent.heading: Remaining terms
mathematicalContent.body: Lrest combines trace times radial derivative, formal Laplacian variation, and the factor (k/2−ℓ) multiplying the trace Taylor product.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines the abstract syntax of constants, jets, trace symbols, formal differential operators, and arithmetic operations used in the recursion.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: E::=0\mid1\mid q\mid k\mid f\mid F_m\mid\operatorname{tr}_j\mid E+E\mid-E\mid EE\mid D_j(E).

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Represents rational scalar multiplication inside the syntax tree.
Casts the natural factorial into ℚ.
Recursively adds a list of formal expressions, with zero for the empty list.

correspondence.leanType: def rangeSum (m : ℕ) (A : ℕ → FormalExpr) : FormalExpr
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Builds the formal sum indexed by j=0,…,m.
Defines the coefficient of the distinguished highest normal jet Fₘ₊₁ in Lₘ.
Defines the trace-times-radial-derivative part; it vanishes at m=0.
Defines the formal Laplacian-variation part of Lₘ.
Represents the scalar factor k/2−ℓ as formal syntax.
Defines the portion of Lₘ excluding the distinguished Fₘ₊₁ term.
Defines the complete formal coefficient equation.

references.locator: Formal Juhl-type formula and OR bridge
references.note: Primary source for the intended curved OR–Juhl identity.
Source for the Juhl side of the formal syntax.

limitations: FormalExpr has no semantics, algebraic quotient, or connection to an actual metric jet in this file. The formulas are syntax trees; equality is syntactic unless a later normalization or interpretation is supplied.
